Hello, I'm Dr. Robert Geldini. Welcome to my official online on-demand program. I'm delighted that you've chosen to embark on a level of learning that will assist you in becoming a more skilled practitioner of influence. Throughout this course, we will be taking an in-depth look at proven research on the seven universal principles of persuasion. From the outset, we have built into the course three main differentiators that separate it from other influence or persuasion programs. First, it is based entirely on properly conducted scientific research into what increases the likelihood that people will say yes to a request. We're not going to be talking about hunches or speculations or war stories. Now we'll be including stories and narratives and examples, but we're not going to base our conclusions on those. We're going to base our conclusion, and I'll make a commitment to you now. We're going to base our conclusions entirely on research that documents the power of particular influence strategies and principles. The second differentiator is that our program focuses on influence approaches that are not only effective, but are also entirely ethical. Look, no one wants to be tricked or misled or in some other way move to do something that they didn't really want to do because they were misguided. If they see that, they never want to return to any influence agent who tried to move them in that direction. We're going to equip you with influence strategies and tactics and approaches that are not only effective, but ethical as well, so both sides gain. As a result, those people who have gained from their interactions with you will want to return to do business in the future with you. It's a perfect marriage of ethics and effectiveness. One last differentiator. The program is designed to do more than merely educate you about the principles. This isn't a college course. No. We're going to demonstrate how to implement that knowledge to increase your persuasive success. That's the key. The key is to be able to move people in your direction, not just because you know about the principles of influence, but you know how to harness them. You know how to apply them. You know how to implement them in ways that move those individuals in your directions.
